solution.p: We will factor x^{2}-11x+30 where a=1, b=-11, and c=30.
Mental Method: When we factor a polynomial in the form of x^2+bx+c, x^2+bxy+cy^2, or x^2y^2+bxy+c we can use mental math to factor. We need to find two numbers whose product is c, and whose sum is b.

For x^{2}-11x+30, since the product of -5 and -6 is 30, and the sum of -5 and -6 is -11, the factorization is \begin{aligned}[t]x^{2}-11x+30\amp =\left(x-5\right)\mathopen{}\left(x-6\right)\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (1) \cdot (30) = 30.

The number 30 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(30)\amp -1(-30)\amp 2(15)\amp -2(-15)
ewline3(10)\amp -3(-10)\amp 5(6)\amp -5(-6)
ewline\end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of -11, and this sum happens with (-6)+(-5)=-11 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]x^{2}-11x+30\amp =x^{2}-6x-5x+30
ewline \amp =x\mathopen{}\left(x-6ight)-5\mathopen{}\left(x-6ight)
ewline \amp =\left(x-5ight)\mathopen{}\left(x-6ight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in x^{2}-11x+30 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 1, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(1)\amp \end{array}

The third term, 30, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(30)\amp -1(-30)\amp 2(15)\amp -2(-15)\newline3(10)\amp -3(-10)\amp 5(6)\amp -5(-6)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with x^{2}-11x+30 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]x^{2}-11x+30\amp =\left(x-5\right)\mathopen{}\left(x-6\right)\end{aligned}
